_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control narratives, policy documentation, audit trails, and supporting artifacts that demonstrate compliance to examiner standards.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Skill RC-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-005 (TF, cosine: 0.487), AB-K-005 (AB, cosine: 0.445).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.554). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. NICE-only terms [conducting, events, test] vs WIDAI-only terms [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.872 (P=0.905, R=0.842). P exceeds R by 0.063 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in conducting test events" and "Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control n". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.494 < STS 0.554 (gap -0.060). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [conducting, events, test] and [artifacts, audit, compliance] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [skill]. NICE-only: [conducting, events, test]. WIDAI-only: [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation].
